title: elaeocarpus floribundus var. tahanensis
intro: Elaeocarpus floribundus var. tahanensis, also known as the Tahan Blue Oak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. It is a plant native to peninsular Malaysia, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The Tahan Blue Oak has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as fever, respiratory problems, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, making it a valuable resource for natural health remedies.
